arcgis_rest_zoning.schema_drift_note: LAM_ID does not exist in the EPI_Primary_Planning_Layers MapServer. Candidate natural keys in the source: OBJECTID (numeric, per-feature), PCO_REF_KEY (string, planning-instrument scoped). Recommend updating HARMONY_P2_ENTITY_SCHEMAS.md zoning_area dedup strategy to use PCO_REF_KEY (or OBJECTID + EPI_NAME composite) in place of LAM_ID.

wfs_cadastre.access_method: ArcGIS REST (SIX) — WFS endpoints returned 400 on GetCapabilities across all candidates

required_query_params.note: inSR=4326 is REQUIRED — layer's native SR is Web Mercator (102100). Without inSR the bbox silently matches 0 features.

schema_drift.shapestarea: actual field is 'shape_Area' (capital A) — mapping update required
schema_drift.plantype: not a separate field — derive from planlabel prefix (e.g. 'DP787786' → plan_type='DP')
schema_drift.plantype_alternative: classsubtype numeric code is available if a coded representation is preferred

issues: Both WFS endpoint candidates (maps.six / portal.spatial) returned 400 on GetCapabilities — WFS is not publicly exposed. ArcGIS REST is the working alternative.

planning_portal_da.error_observed: { "ErrorMessage": "Required parameters for OnlineDA endpoint is not met." }

planning_portal_cdc.error_observed: { "ErrorMessage": "Required parameters for OnlineCDC endpoint is not met." }

recommendations: ENTITY SCHEMA UPDATE REQUIRED: zoning_area.LAM_ID does not exist in source. Update HARMONY_P2_ENTITY_SCHEMAS.md to map source_feature_id from PCO_REF_KEY (or composite OBJECTID + EPI_NAME). Layer 2 of EPI_Primary_Planning_Layers confirmed as Land Zoning.

ENTITY SCHEMA UPDATE REQUIRED: cadastral_lot.shapestarea → shape_Area (case change). cadastral_lot.plantype → derive from planlabel prefix (e.g. DP787786 → 'DP'). No WFS adapter required — use ArcGIS REST adapter with inSR=4326.
ArcGIS REST adapter MUST implement pagination (max 1000 records per query confirmed empirically).
OSM building height/levels coverage low (height=0.0%, levels=15.0%). Accept for MVP per Gap #8 in pillar brief.
